metadata.description: Spark is a lending protocol built on an Aave V3 fork (SparkLend) with an integrated savings layer that routes deposits through the Sky (formerly MakerDAO) DAI Savings Rate (DSR) via the Pot contract. Governance decisions execute through the Sky governance stack — DSPause enforces a 24-hour delay before DSPauseProxy forwards calls to SubProxy, which holds admin rights on every SparkLend contract. An Asset Liability Management (ALM) layer controlled by three separate multisigs lets the protocol rebalance capital via arbitrary external calls. Price data comes from a mix of Chainlink, Chronicle, RedStone, and protocol-specific exchange-rate oracles for liquid staking and restaking tokens. The protocol currently secures $3.66B across SparkLend markets and the savings module.

admins.name: DSPauseProxy (Sky Governance Executor)
admins.description: The execution endpoint of the Sky governance stack. DSPause plots a governance spell here after the 24-hour delay, and DSPauseProxy then delegatecalls it — effectively routing every governance action against Spark contracts. It is the root admin of SubProxy, SDAO, ESM, and StarGuard. Because every upgrade and parameter change in SparkLend ultimately resolves to this contract, it controls $3.66B in reachable capital and the $360.75M SPK token supply.

admins.name: ESM (Emergency Shutdown Module)
admins.description: Sky's Emergency Shutdown Module. When enough MKR/SKY is burned into the ESM to cross a configured threshold, it can call `exec` on SubProxy to trigger Sky governance. In Spark's permission graph it appears as an admin of SubProxy, but it is only activated during a system-wide Sky emergency shutdown.

admins.name: StarGuard (Sky Authority Registry)
admins.description: Sky's authority contract registered as admin of SubProxy via the `can` access-control pattern. It whitelists which addresses may call which functions on SubProxy, and is itself administered by DSPauseProxy.

mitigations.label: spell-delay
mitigations.description: Invocation via StarGuard (SubDAO governance) requires a spell to be plotted on StarGuard and executed within the maxDelay window (7 days). Each spell additionally enforces its own `isExecutable()` off-chain delay (commonly a timestamp gate) before StarGuard.exec can delegatecall through SubProxy.

admins.name: SubProxy (Sky Governance Relay)
admins.description: Owned by DSPauseProxy and registered as the admin, owner, or `PoolAdmin`/`EmergencyAdmin` role-holder on virtually every Spark contract. Through its 116 permissioned entry points it can add/drop reserves, change interest rate strategies, upgrade ATokens via PoolConfigurator, change the price oracle, reassign access-control roles, and move treasury funds in Collector. Controls $3.66B in directly held contracts and $3.66B in reachable capital — essentially the full protocol.

mitigations.label: bounded mint
mitigations.description: Unbacked mints are capped per reserve by the `unbackedMintCap` configuration (settable only by POOL_ADMIN via PoolConfigurator.setUnbackedMintCap). Total outstanding unbacked supply cannot exceed the cap: `require(unbacked <= unbackedMintCap * 10**decimals)`. A reserve with `unbackedMintCap == 0` disables unbacked minting entirely.

mitigations.label: rewards-only
mitigations.description: Impact is contained to the protocol's incentives system (wstETH reward emissions). User deposits, withdrawals, and the underlying aToken lending positions are unaffected — the lending-side handleAction hook on ATokens is pointed by POOL_ADMIN, not by EmissionManager. The reward distribution uses a PullRewardsTransferStrategy (0x11aAC1cA..) that pulls wstETH from the Rewards Vault (2/3 GnosisSafe 0x8076...) via transferFrom on each claim. Neither the RewardsController nor the TransferStrategy itself custodies reward tokens. The cap is wired to `EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance` (a live on-chain read of the Safe's wstETH balance), so it updates automatically as the vault is topped up or drained.

mitigations.label: per-user reward theft
mitigations.description: setClaimer authorizes a third party to call claimRewardsOnBehalf(user, to=arbitrary), redirecting the targeted user's unclaimed accrued rewards anywhere. Bounded per call to that user's unclaimed balance, and system-wide to the Vault's live wstETH balance. Deposits, aTokens, and other users' rewards cannot be reached without a separate setClaimer call per victim.

mitigations.label: reward-escalation
mitigations.description: Grants full control of the reward system (per-reward emissionAdmin or EmissionManager owner). All downstream actions remain contained to the reward system; final drainable value is bounded by the Rewards Vault's live wstETH balance.

mitigations.label: orphan pipe
mitigations.description: Only rewires the EmissionManager's internal pointer to a RewardsController. The ATokens keep their existing IncentivesController (set via POOL_ADMIN on each AToken), so existing accrual continues to be tracked on the old controller and remains fully claimable. Future emission configuration changes via EmissionManager will simply not reach the ATokens — a misconfiguration, not a fund-impact event.

mitigations.label: bounded retroactive
mitigations.description: configureAssets re-checkpoints the distribution using a caller-supplied `totalSupply` (not read on-chain), so a malicious emissionAdmin can retroactively skew accruals for the interval since the last checkpoint. Effect is bounded to emissionPerSecond × time-delta, and ultimately to the Vault's wstETH balance (tracked as EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance). setEmissionPerSecond reads scaledTotalSupply() on-chain and is not exploitable this way.

mitigations.label: claim-DoS
mitigations.description: Replacing the TransferStrategy does not transfer the old strategy's transferFrom allowance, so a malicious replacement cannot pull the Vault's wstETH unless the Safe signs a fresh approve. Worst-case is DoS on future claims (replacement strategy reverts or has zero allowance); already-accrued amounts persist and become claimable once the pipe is restored. Bounded by the Vault's live wstETH balance if a new allowance is granted.

Existing borrow positions, collateral deposits, repayments, and withdrawals remain fully functional — only new borrow origination is blocked.

admins.name: SparkLendFreezerMom (Emergency Freeze Contract)
admins.description: An immutable protocol contract owned by the 3/5 Emergency Freezer multisig. It is registered as `EmergencyAdmin` and `RiskAdmin` on the ACLManager, letting it bypass standard governance delays to freeze or pause SparkLend markets. It is a relay — the real authority is the owner multisig.

admins.name: PoolAddressesProvider (SparkLend Upgrader)
admins.description: Immutable registry contract owned by SubProxy. It is the direct admin of the Pool and PoolConfigurator EIP-1967 proxies — calling `setPoolImpl`/`setPoolConfiguratorImpl` triggers the proxy upgrade. Effective authority therefore sits with Sky governance through SubProxy.

mitigations.label: bounded-by-spark-rsETH-pool

admins.name: Emission Admin 2/3 Multisig
admins.description: A 2-of-3 GnosisSafe registered as the emission admin on the EmissionManager. It configures reward distribution parameters — `configureAssets`, `setTransferStrategy`, `setRewardOracle`, `setDistributionEnd`, `setEmissionPerSecond` — affecting a limited reward-distribution scope ($78.72K in reachable capital). Cannot affect collateral or debt markets.

mitigations.label: rewards-only
mitigations.description: Impact is contained to the protocol's incentives system (wstETH reward emissions). User deposits, withdrawals, and the underlying aToken lending positions are unaffected — the lending-side handleAction hook on ATokens is pointed by POOL_ADMIN, not by EmissionManager. The reward distribution uses a PullRewardsTransferStrategy (0x11aAC1cA..) that pulls wstETH from the Rewards Vault (2/3 GnosisSafe 0x8076...) via transferFrom on each claim. Neither the RewardsController nor the TransferStrategy itself custodies reward tokens. The cap is wired to `EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance` (a live on-chain read of the Safe's wstETH balance), so it updates automatically as the vault is topped up or drained.

mitigations.label: bounded retroactive
mitigations.description: configureAssets re-checkpoints the distribution using a caller-supplied `totalSupply` (not read on-chain), so a malicious emissionAdmin can retroactively skew accruals for the interval since the last checkpoint. Effect is bounded to emissionPerSecond × time-delta, and ultimately to the Vault's wstETH balance (tracked as EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance). setEmissionPerSecond reads scaledTotalSupply() on-chain and is not exploitable this way.

mitigations.label: rewards-only
mitigations.description: Impact is contained to the protocol's incentives system (wstETH reward emissions). User deposits, withdrawals, and the underlying aToken lending positions are unaffected — the lending-side handleAction hook on ATokens is pointed by POOL_ADMIN, not by EmissionManager. The reward distribution uses a PullRewardsTransferStrategy (0x11aAC1cA..) that pulls wstETH from the Rewards Vault (2/3 GnosisSafe 0x8076...) via transferFrom on each claim. Neither the RewardsController nor the TransferStrategy itself custodies reward tokens. The cap is wired to `EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance` (a live on-chain read of the Safe's wstETH balance), so it updates automatically as the vault is topped up or drained.

mitigations.label: claim-DoS
mitigations.description: Replacing the TransferStrategy does not transfer the old strategy's transferFrom allowance, so a malicious replacement cannot pull the Vault's wstETH unless the Safe signs a fresh approve. Worst-case is DoS on future claims (replacement strategy reverts or has zero allowance); already-accrued amounts persist and become claimable once the pipe is restored. Bounded by the Vault's live wstETH balance if a new allowance is granted.

admins.name: ALM Controller 1/2 Multisig
admins.description: A 1-of-2 Safe registered as a controller on ALMProxyFreezable, giving it `doCall`, `doCallWithValue`, and `doDelegateCall` — the ability to execute arbitrary calls and delegatecalls from the ALM proxy. The ALM proxy is a controller on 23 downstream contracts reaching $3.66B in capital; the 1-of-2 threshold means a single signer can trigger these calls.

admins.name: ALM Controller 2/5 Multisig
admins.description: A 2-of-5 Safe registered as a second controller on ALMProxyFreezable with the same arbitrary-call powers as the 1/2 Safe — `doCall`, `doCallWithValue`, `doDelegateCall` — reaching $3.66B in downstream capital. Two signers out of five are sufficient.

admins.name: ALM Controller Manager 1/4 Multisig
admins.description: A 1-of-4 Safe that can call `removeController` on ALMProxyFreezable, removing the other ALM multisigs from the controller set. A single signer is sufficient to execute this action, affecting $3.66B in reachable capital.

admins.name: Sky Governance Chief (DSPause Owner)
admins.description: The on-chain voting contract that owns DSPause and is authorised to plot and drop governance spells. Control is held by MKR/SKY token voters through the Sky governance system; any address with sufficient delegated votes can propose actions, which then execute after the 24-hour DSPause delay. From Spark's perspective this is the ultimate source of governance authority.

mitigations.description: Every authority-gated action is subject to the 1-day timelock: plot() requires eta >= block.timestamp + delay, and plan execution is deferred until eta.

admins.name: DSPause (Sky Governance Timelock)
admins.description: Immutable contract enforcing a hardcoded 86,400-second (24-hour) delay on every governance action before it can execute. Spells are plotted by the DSPause owner (the Sky/MKR voting Chief), wait 24 hours, and are then fired through DSPauseProxy. The delay itself can be changed only by executing a spell through DSPause.

Both paths are bounded by Spark's rsETH pool balance (tracked as LRTOracle.sparkRsETHPoolBalance — currently 15.32 rsETH ≈ $38K).

dependencies.name: Chronicle Aggor ETH/USD
dependencies.description: Immutable aggregator that returns the median of three independent ETH/USD feeds — Chainlink, Chronicle, and RedStone — after filtering out stale (>25h) and invalid responses. A single compromised provider is sandwiched out by the median; with only two healthy feeds it returns the average; with one healthy feed it returns that single value with no fallback. Used directly for the WETH reserve and indirectly (via ratio oracles) for wstETH, rETH, weETH, ezETH, and rsETH. Oracle addresses are hardcoded as constants — neither Chronicle Labs nor Spark can swap a feed without a redeploy. Note: SparkLend's POOL_ADMIN can still bypass this protection by repointing `assetSources` away from the Aggor.

mitigations.label: rewards-only
mitigations.description: Impact is contained to the protocol's incentives system (wstETH reward emissions). User deposits, withdrawals, and the underlying aToken lending positions are unaffected — the lending-side handleAction hook on ATokens is pointed by POOL_ADMIN, not by EmissionManager. The reward distribution uses a PullRewardsTransferStrategy (0x11aAC1cA..) that pulls wstETH from the Rewards Vault (2/3 GnosisSafe 0x8076...) via transferFrom on each claim. Neither the RewardsController nor the TransferStrategy itself custodies reward tokens. The cap is wired to `EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance` (a live on-chain read of the Safe's wstETH balance), so it updates automatically as the vault is topped up or drained.

mitigations.label: bounded retroactive
mitigations.description: configureAssets re-checkpoints the distribution using a caller-supplied `totalSupply` (not read on-chain), so a malicious emissionAdmin can retroactively skew accruals for the interval since the last checkpoint. Effect is bounded to emissionPerSecond × time-delta, and ultimately to the Vault's wstETH balance (tracked as EmissionManager.rewardsVaultWstETHBalance). setEmissionPerSecond reads scaledTotalSupply() on-chain and is not exploitable this way.

mitigations.label: bounded-by-spark-rsETH-pool
mitigations.description: Any Kelp-side compromise (LRTOracle price manipulation, rsETH exchange rate fraud) can only affect Spark up to the rsETH collateral held by the Spark lending pool. Over-valuing rsETH lets attackers over-borrow against it (drain other assets up to rsETH-collateral × LTV); under-valuing unfairly liquidates rsETH-collateralized positions. Both paths are bounded by Spark's rsETH pool balance (tracked as LRTOracle.sparkRsETHPoolBalance — currently 15.32 rsETH ≈ $38K).
